In December of 2016 I made a terrible choice and decided to drive home from a Christmas Party after drinking. I was pulled over for speeding and arrested for DWI. I was taken to Harris County Jail and spent the next 36 hours shuffling from cell to cell wondering what permanent damage I had done to my life.

On recommendation my wife contacted Thomas Locke to see if he could assist me. After being released from jail Thomas met with us on a Saturday morning to discuss my options and explain the process to me. Being that I had never been arrested this was extremely helpful. Also, it was reassuring to speak with someone who was not judgmental about the mistake I had made.

Over the next several months there were many legal challenges for my case. Thomas was by my side at every hearing and working furiously on my behalf to help me see the resolution of my case in an expeditious manner and the least long-term effects to my criminal record. This included getting me into the Pretrial Diversion Program despite my not meeting the criteria for enrollment.

In July of this year I will got to court for the final time ending this nightmare. The charges against me will be dismissed and soon after the arrest will disappear from my record and it will be as if none of this ever happened.

There is no doubt in my mind that Thomas Locke saved my life and there is no other attorney I would want by my side.

reviews.text: Leena and her Law Offices team are simply described as *excellent*.  We applied for my mother's permanent residency and in less than 3 months, my mother received her green card.  Our case was extremely straight-forward and without any anticipated hiccups.  Nevertheless, Leena and her team ensured that we left no stone unturned. A fairly common occurrence among immigrants from many countries and of many stripes is that the child is named only ~ 7-10 days after birth and following some other religious / spiritual ceremony and consequently, the child's official name does not appear in the LIVE certificate of birth alongside the mother's name. Leena and her team diligently walked us through the process of providing additional supporting and validating documents, (such as the School Leaving certificate, domicile certificates from the original country of residence, other affidavits).  Leena and her team took the time to describe and detail the "how" and the "what" in terms of the substance of these supporting documents and testaments.  In my opinion, the speed of Green Card processing was in no small measure due to Leena and her team's diligence and attention to detail.  Thanks again for all your responsiveness, help and support through this process.

reviews.text:
Hatchett Law Firm was fine… until I questioned them about possibly overcharging me for email exchanges that I believe were due to disorganization of their company. They literally charged me *hundreds of dollars* - AFTER probate court was over (when I had already paid a big retainer PLUS some) for email exchanges that shouldn't have happened in the first place. Everything was fine enough and I had already overlooked what I felt were other overcharges, until they emailed me after probate to ask if I wanted original copies of my documents. When I said yes, they replied that I actually already had original copies - then charged me for the whole interaction. Why should I pay you for emails your law firm sent that never needed to be sent in the first place?? If I already have original copies, you should know, and then not ask me if I want my original copies. And then it is *ludacris* to charge me for the email exchange your firm started to figure this out!

How this started: Less than a week ago, I reviewed my invoice and then questioned the receptionist about charges on my bill. Within TWO DAYS of me calling to ask them about this, and letting them know I would like to talk to someone about it, and then not hearing a response from them re: my overcharge concerns - they simply texted me and claimed that they were going to "notify the court of my decision” not to pay them! When it has been single digit days from when I was even in probate court with them, to then being given an additional bill (after I was told that the last additional $1k I paid them, in addition to my first retainer, would surely be enough to carry me through to the end of my case - only to get another bill for $2k), and then this interaction!

This firm immediately flipped on me, and I am beyond disturbed by their unprofessional behavior. I have dealt with numerous attorneys in my life, and NEVER have I ever had a firm behave this unprofessionally. Ever. And never have I ever had a firm attack me for taking more than a few days to pay a $2k bill from the time I got the invoice, with zero evidence I was refusing to pay?? Maybe if you give people enough time to pay, YOU WOULD KNOW THAT!

I initially wrote a good review one week ago - because as soon as I left their office after notarizing probate court documents, they immediately asked if I would write a review and handed me a paper with a QR code on it. Now I can see that if I had waited until I got home to write a review (I had to fly from Oregon to Texas, and as soon as I got home I had to manage my many affairs and responsibilities, as I am a super single mom with two kids and two dogs), I probably would have seen their organizations true colors and written a more accurate review. Like this one.
reviews.ownerResponse:
We take client feedback seriously. Because this matter involves a former client, our ability to respond in detail is limited by our ethical duty of confidentiality.

What we can clarify is this: the work requested in this probate matter was completed. Following the court hearing, additional required tasks were performed, including preparation and filing of post-hearing documents necessary to assist her with estate administration, including fees we advanced to the court and publications from our firm account on her behalf. Those services were reflected on a final amount due of $756 — not $2,000 as stated in this review.

This client previously posted a positive review immediately after her hearing, expressing satisfaction with our services. Specifically, she wrote then "Attorney Mike Hatchett, as well as the entire team, were so wonderful and professional during the entire process. They handled my case with care and compassion and kindness and I really cannot thank them enough for how they helped me. I would recommend them to anyone!"

The concerns raised here arose only after she received a reminder to pay her final invoice for the services she received and the expenses we paid on her behalf. When a client disputes the service, indicates an unwillingness to pay, and threatens posting negative feedback in retaliation, we are required to formally acknowledge termination of representation and notify the court that we no longer represent the party. The only "flipping" that occured was from the client who we extended service and credit without sufficient retainer, who when asked to settle her account that it would be acceptable to attack the professionals that she previously praised and threaten to post a negative review if she did not get her way. This is not about a "possible overcharge," we can handle a billing disagreement without it being a dispute. This is about decency.

We do not charge for unnecessary communications. All billing entries reflect time spent responding to client inquiries, completing required filings, and administering the estate through completion. Like most law firms, we bill for attorney and staff time as outlined in our engagement agreement. We understand that legal services are an investment and that final invoices can feel stressful. However, suggesting disorganization or retaliation mischaracterizes what occurred. No services were withheld. The matter was brought through hearing and required filings were completed.

We appreciate that many clients find us through the reviews posted here. We understand the damage to reputation caused by untrue reviews, like the one we are now addressing. However, we will not allow anyone to threaten use of a negative review to negotiate payment of their bills. We trust the public will discern the truth. What is not being said is that her work was done and we are left holding her unpaid bill.

We remain committed to serving our community with professionalism, transparency, and adherence to ethical standards. While we regret this one person chose to discard the relationship over what she now states is a "possible overcharge for messages", we stand behind the work performed and the integrity of our billing practices. For prospective clients reading this: we are looking to build long-term relationships and always happy to discuss billing expectations and scope of representation at the outset of a matter and throughout a matter so there are no surprises and we remain aligned to get the work done.

My experience with Cynthia Hatchett was extremely difficult and unprofessional. Communication was emotionally charged, and attempts to address concerns or hold her or her staff accountable were treated as personal attacks rather than client feedback.

I would not recommend her.
reviews.ownerResponse:
A negative review left anonymously that attempts to address professional accountability is ironic. According to google "True Everybody" hasn't written any reviews yet, or has chosen not to show them on their profile.

We appreciate all feedback and remain committed to integrity, transparency, and respectful service to those we represent. Any party seeking legal representation, especially in a contested matter, should prepare to abide by the terms and conditions of representation which include financial responsibility, responsive communication, cooperation in case management and truthful disclosures, or expect the lawyer or firm will not continue representation.

Probate litigation and mediation are usually complex and emotionally taxing because it involves a dispute with family members. At all times our clients receive clear communication about fees and firm expectations. The attorney-client relationship is a relationship. When a client has no respect for the relationship, it should be expected that the relationship ends. Your review indicates that you were advised multiple times that the relationship should end and why. There were no threats - only truthful communication, not hidden behind anonymous profile. Ultimately, we achieved your objectives at mediation and advised that if a breach of the attorney-client agreement occurred again, the withdraw would happen. The timing of the withdraw was a result of your actions and made in good faith and in compliance with our professional duties. The engagement ended several months ago with you finding a new lawyer to meet your needs.

While this post may have been intended for reputational harm, we have faith that it will deliver a result that informs prospective clients seeking representation during the most difficult time in life. We value the attorney-client relationship above all aspects of the practice of law. We have courage to stand with our clients in tough spaces and we support our clients fully with integrity. Trust matters. Sometimes we have to deliver hard truths and set boundaries with clients, no matter the consequences - including receiving the feedback of an untruthful/one-sided anonymous review here and there.

reviews.text:
I do not recommend Attorney Cynthia Hatchett’s law firm based on our experience handling my mother’s probate case.

My sister hired Attorney Hatchett to represent our family during a very difficult time. Communication became an issue early on—when we asked questions about the status of the case, we were met with threats that she would stop working with us. After more than 18 months, only limited progress had been made.

Following mediation, Attorney Hatchett scheduled our case for a trial, which was unnecessary. The court later canceled the trial because a trial was not required; the case should have been set for a prove-up instead. When we asked for clarification about why this occurred, Attorney Hatchett stated she would no longer work with us.

Although she said she was withdrawing from the case, she never formally withdrew and stopped working altogether—after having been paid a substantial amount in legal fees.

We ultimately hired a new attorney who immediately stepped in, charged a far more reasonable fee, and was surprised by how our case had been handled. The contrast in professionalism and integrity was significant.

While Attorney Hatchett does have a capable support staff, based on our experience, we do not recommend this firm.
reviews.ownerResponse:
Ms. Thomas,
Thank you for sharing your perspective. This response is to clarify that our firm make effort to practice with integrity, clear boundaries, and adherence to professional standards, even when doing so is unpopular, misunderstood, or wholly disregarded by a client or their family.

Several statements in your review materially mischaracterize both our role and the procedural history of the case. Because we are bound by strict duties of confidentiality, we cannot publicly address many of the assertions made here or disclose communications, strategy, or advice that would provide full context.

To begin, our firm was not retained to represent the family as a whole, and as you know, we expressly declined to represent you personally.

With respect to the procedural posture of the case, the characterization
of events is also inaccurate. A trial setting for your sister's case was in place well before mediation. The trial was set by the court’s docket control order and not the firm. Our client was fully aware of how and why the case progressed and we will not reveal client confidences or case strategy here.

It is wholly false that the trial was “unnecessary” or “canceled by the court”; rather, the successful mediation rendered a trial unnecessary because the case settled and was removed from the court's trial docket, which is precisely how the process is intended to work.

Once the objectives being pursued were not aligned with the terms of representation or with our professional judgment, we encouraged the retention of alternate counsel and provided time for that transition so that interests could be protected. We cannot ethically continue representation when a client will not abide by the agreed terms or when the attorney–client relationship has broken down. We are genuinely pleased that you believe your sister is now well represented and that the matter has moved forward.

We wish your family peace and resolution moving forward.

We were referred to Ross Phillips by Brazoria County to sign and file some legal documents.  My 87 year old mother wanted to get her affairs in order and list myself and my siblings as beneficiaries of her estate.
After the documents were filed, my brother called his office because he was confused as to why they were mailed to him instead of to our mother.  He found Ross to be dismissive and rude.
My mother and brother then suggested that I email Ross to clarify the issues that we were all confused about.  So I emailed him but never received a  response.  So after a month, I called and spoke to him.  He told me that he explained everything in the initial visit and saw no need to respond to my simple email asking for clarification.
I found him to be exactly as my brother described.  He talked down to me, was dismissive of my questions, and very rude and disrespectful.
My mother was a paying client and our family does NOT recommend this firm!

I made an appointment for my 75 yr old disabled mother with Mr Phillips. I made an appointment with Mr Phillips to see my mother. She no longer drives so she had to get someone to drive her to her appt. She arrived at her appt and Mr Phillips was unable to make the appt. She was told by his office when she arrived that he couldn't make the appt and he must have written her phone number down wrong. So he was unable to reach her to let her know. Totally understandable. Unfortunately Mr Phillips son was in the office and he came to speak to my mom and her driver. He then preceded to berate my mother and bring her to tears. He told her in so many ways what she was trying to do wasn't possible. Then while she is crying he then tells her she should pay him since he is $250 an hr and his dad was $500. My mom and her driver then left shocked on what they just witnessed.
I then phoned Mr Phillips a few days later about what happen since my mom was so upset. I spoke to Mr Phillips and he said he apologized for writing the number down wrong and would talk to his son then reach out to my mom. Weeks have gone by and not a word from Mr Phillips. Very disappointed as Mr Phillips was recommended by friends, and I wanted to support a local business since i had lived in Pearland over 20 yrs. I did not want to write this review but after I read some of the other reviews I needed to share. Mr Phillips had plenty of time to make this right after I reached out to him.

That was the reason for me calling in the first place to set up an appointment for someone to get those options in the first place. I finally said I would get back to them.  So I guess if you are a realestate attorney and already know what to do these people would be glad to do it, but if you dont know then oh well I guess find you another realstate attorney :) hahaha Maybe they were having a bad day? We thought "okay, no big deal" but it was because several weeks later we received a Medicaid cancellation notice because he was no longer on SSI. Repeated trips to local Medicaid offices yielded more forms to fill out: "try this one" we heard more than once. Enter the knowledgeable and magnanimous Les Berry. At the consultation meeting with him he assembled all the facts and stated that our son was indeed eligible for Medicaid, without trying to sell his services. reviews.text:
We had recently moved from a different state and wanted to update our trust according to Texas law. Our previous lawyer informed us we would just need a simple amendment and it shouldn’t cost us very much. After meeting with an assistant at this company (not an actual lawyer), we were told they’d have to redo our entire trust to the tune of $5000-$8990 depending on which package we wanted. Red flags went up so we decided to contact our previous lawyer who suggested we contact the Texas State bar and shop around for a different company. Thank goodness we did! Our previous lawyer was correct! In fact, our trust was even more secure from our previous state that we would’ve put ourselves in jeopardy had we followed the advice of this company. For a fraction of the cost, we were able to do a simple amendment and secure our trust here in Texas. This company is very expensive, and inaccurate. Shop around, don’t take their word as gospel truth! I would’ve given one star, but instead gave 2 because the assistant was kind and only doing her job.

UPDATE: Thanks for your reply to my review! I can see how some people may require what you stated if they lacked a sound trust to start with, however, had someone from your team looked up a simple law from our previous state, you’d realize our trust was iron clad compared to Texas law and again we would’ve been charged thousands of dollars unnecessarily. Do not be fooled! Wills and trusts are protected by the constitution and are recognized regardless of which state you move from and to. Do your homework! This company is extremely expensive and inaccurate when it came to our case. A simple check of our previous state law would have confirmed this. It’s also not accurate that you need an entire trust redone - that only benefits this company’s pocket, not the consumer.
reviews.ownerResponse:
Thank you for sharing your feedback. We’re sorry to hear that our approach didn’t align with your expectations but are happy that you found someone to do what you were looking for. Every family’s situation is unique, and our recommendations are based on Texas law and the comprehensive planning our clients have come to rely on. Our goal is to provide every option to clients so they can make an informed decision about what is best for them.

At our firm, we do not amend estate plans drafted by other attorneys. This is a deliberate decision made to avoid potential legal and liability issues especially since most plans do not meet our standards. Instead, we offer full restatements that allow us to ensure your plan is not only legally sound in Texas, but also aligned with what most of our clients are truly looking for: peace of mind that their loved ones will be taken care of, their assets protected, and court involvement avoided.

Many of our clients come to us because they want to plan for the possibility of long-term care needs, including protecting assets while qualifying for benefits like Medicaid. Others want to ensure their families can avoid costly and time-consuming court processes such as probate and guardianship. In our experience, these goals cannot be reliably accomplished through a simple amendment—especially when the laws vary so much from state to state.

While we understand our services may not be the right fit for everyone, we are committed to providing clear guidance and high-quality legal solutions that go beyond “bare minimum” planning. We appreciate your kind words about our team member and wish you the very best moving forward.

If you’re reading this review and want to ensure your plan truly protects your family, your future, and your legacy—we invite you to schedule a strategy session with our team to learn what your options are. We’re here to help!

We take all feedback seriously and are genuinely sorry to hear that your experience with our firm did not meet your expectations. However, we must respectfully clarify and respond to the concerns you’ve raised.

Our firm is committed to delivering high-quality legal representation, and we pride ourselves on maintaining transparency and continuity in every client relationship. While we acknowledge that attorney transitions can occur—due to scheduling, specialization, or other internal considerations—we make every effort to ensure that such transitions do not disrupt the progress or integrity of a client’s case. In no instance is a client ever charged to “start over.” All work performed on a matter remains part of the case, regardless of which attorney is leading it.

As for communication with our leadership, we regret if your son felt he did not receive a timely response. We have established channels for client concerns and review every request thoroughly. If any attempt to reach us was missed or not responded to as expected, we welcome the opportunity to revisit that and make it right.

While we cannot speak to the specifics of your son’s case in a public forum due to confidentiality, we would be more than willing to discuss any remaining concerns directly. Our goal has always been and continues to be achieving the best possible outcomes for those we represent.

reviews.text:
When you need a lawyer, you hire THE BEST, and Tad Nelson & Associates are absolutely that — no question about it!!!

Over Christmas, my entire life was turned upside down by false allegations, which led to misdemeanor charges and a protective order. I was scared, overwhelmed, and honestly didn’t know how I was going to get through it. I called Tad Nelson & Associates Christmas Eve.

From the very beginning, Tad went above and beyond. He gave me his personal cell phone number and answered every call and every text, even during the holidays and on weekends. I never once felt ignored or brushed off — I felt like I mattered.

That Saturday, I met with Tad to complete paperwork. He told me that he and his team would do everything they could to get me home by New Year’s Eve so I could see my family.

On Monday morning, Tad and Amber were already at the courthouse by 8:00 AM, fighting hard to have the charges dropped.

By 1:00 PM that same day, Tad called me and told me my charges were COMPLETELY DISMISSED. I collapsed. I dropped to the ground crying in disbelief. One phone call changed everything — fear, panic, and uncertainty turned into overwhelming relief.

But they weren’t done yet. The false protective order was still keeping me from my family. Tad and Amber refused to stop. Amber fought relentlessly, pushing the court again and again so I could go home.

On New Year’s Eve at 6:30 PM, both Tad and Amber personally called me and told me the words I will never forget: “The orders are signed! You can go home!”

Without Tad Nelson and Amber, I would have been separated from my family for 90 days. Because of them, it took less than 7. They didn’t just do their jobs — they fought for me, believed in me, and gave me my life back.

The love, respect, dedication, and heart that Tad and Amber put into their work is beyond words. Tad and Amber, I know you’re reading this — and I need you to know that I am forever grateful. Thank you for fighting for me when I couldn’t fight for myself. Thank you for bringing me home.

If you are facing criminal charges and feel scared, lost, or alone — call Tad Nelson & Associates. Period.
reviews.ownerResponse:
Thank you for sharing such a heartfelt and powerful review. It truly means more than words can express. From the moment you reached out, we knew how urgent and personal this situation was, and our entire focus was on protecting you and getting you back where you belonged, with your family.

Cases like yours remind us why we do this work. When someone is facing false allegations and the system moves too quickly or unfairly, it’s our job to step in, push back, and make sure the truth is heard. Helping you reunite with your child and your family was exactly the outcome we were fighting for, and we’re grateful we were able to do that.

Thank you for trusting us during one of the most difficult moments of your life. We’re honored to have stood beside you, and we wish you nothing but peace and strength moving forward.
- Tad & Amber

reviews.text: Your a lawyer, still part of the system to suppress. Start thinking outside the system and you will do more good that way.
reviews.ownerResponse:
I believe this review is connected to a jury verdict that was returned yesterday in a very serious and emotionally difficult case. I cannot say that with absolute certainty, but given the timing and the nature of the comment, it is a reasonable assumption and my response is written with that understanding in mind.

Cases like this are never easy. When a life has been lost, there is no outcome that truly feels like justice to everyone involved. I fully recognize and deeply respect that the victim’s family is hurting. Their grief is real, and it is something no courtroom result can ever erase. They are in my thoughts and prayers.

As a criminal defense lawyer, my role is not to minimize loss, excuse harm, or ignore pain. My role is to stand between the power of the government and an individual citizen and ensure that the process is fair, lawful, and just. That responsibility exists even when the case is emotionally charged and the stakes are extraordinarily high.

I take that duty seriously. I work hard. I work honestly. And I work relentlessly to ensure that every client receives a defense grounded in the facts, the law, and the Constitution. In this case, my partner Amber and I prepared thoroughly, presented the evidence carefully, and advocated for our client with integrity and respect for the gravity of the situation.

It is important for people reading this to understand a fundamental truth about the justice system: criminal defense attorneys do not decide outcomes. Juries do. Our role is to make sure the jury hears all the evidence and that the law is followed. When a verdict is returned, it reflects the collective judgment of citizens who were tasked with weighing the facts—not indifference to loss or suffering.

I understand that some people will never agree with the work criminal defense lawyers do, no matter how carefully, ethically, or responsibly it is done. That reaction often comes from deep pain, and I accept that as part of this profession.

I could ask for this review to be removed, but I won’t. I believe it is important for the public to see the reality of criminal defense work and the emotional weight that comes with it. One side may feel relief, while another feels anger or grief; and both realities can exist at the same time.

To anyone considering my firm: know that when I take on a case, I do so with seriousness, honesty, and respect for every person involved. I will always work as hard as the law allows, I will always tell the truth, and I will always fight for my client within the bounds of ethics and justice.

And to those who are hurting: I am truly sorry for your pain. I do not take lightly the human cost behind any case. You are in my thoughts and prayers.

— Tad Nelson

A good legal defense isn’t cheap, but If you don’t want to be in jail, call this man. He often offers discounts for paying in full up front or cash, so he will work with you. If you have concerns or feel like you’re out of the loop, call his office and tell them that.
reviews.ownerResponse: Gray, thank you for sharing your experience. I’m really glad to hear you felt confident in the defense and the outcome we achieved together. I also appreciate your honesty about communication - that kind of feedback helps us get better. I’m grateful for the trust you placed in us and happy we could help you come out of a tough situation on the right side of things.
